title: iochroma calycinum
intro: Iochroma calycinum, also known as the purple flower, is a flowering plant species native to South America, particularly in the Andean region. It is a shrub-like plant belonging to the nightshade family, Solanaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, making it useful for treating various health conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. Herbal preparations of Iochroma calycinum are often made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to alleviate symptoms and promote overall well-being.
